topic: My building's elevator stops at floor thirteen every Thursday at seven PM but there is no thirteenth floor
title: My Building's Elevator Stops at Floor 13 Every Thursday at 7 PM But There Is No 13th Floor | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: There is no thirteenth floor in my building. My elevator stops there every Thursday at seven.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an old brass elevator floor button panel with the number thirteen visible but unlit, dim ceiling bulb overhead, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Twelve story walk up in Chicago. Lived here three years.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior of a twelve story brick apartment building in Chicago at dusk, fire escapes on the facade, scattered window lights, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Elevator goes one through twelve. Up. Down.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of an elevator interior brushed metal button panel showing floors one through twelve in a column, dim ambient cab light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last March I was riding home. Thursday. Just after seven.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an elevator wall mounted digital floor indicator showing seven oh four PM, ambient overhead light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Doors opened on a floor that does not exist.
scenes.image_prompt: wide interior of an apartment hallway viewed through opening elevator doors, identical carpet and wallpaper extending into shadow, no people, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Hallway looked like mine. Same carpet. Same wallpaper. The door numbers were wrong.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a hallway wall showing two adjacent door numbers, neither familiar, dim hallway sconce light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Tried again the next Thursday. Stood inside.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a hand pressing an elevator button at hip height, only the hand visible, dim cab interior, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Seven oh four. Buttons three and seven both lit. Doors opened on thirteen.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of an elevator button panel with two buttons illuminated simultaneously, glow visible through dim cab interior, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Asked the doorman. He said the building has never had a thirteenth floor.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a uniformed doorman seen from behind at a wood paneled front desk, his hand on a leather log book, no face visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last week I stepped out. The numbers on the doors counted up to mine.
scenes.image_prompt: wide interior of a long apartment hallway counting up to a final door at the end, numbers visible on each side, no people, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: There was someone moving inside my unit.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a brass apartment door knob and peephole, no figure visible behind the peephole, soft hallway light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I have been taking the stairs since.
scenes.image_prompt: wide interior of a building stairwell with bare concrete steps descending into shadow, single fluorescent fixture, no people, vertical 9:16 framing
